content.text:
Get started with Week 7. Lets go to cloud. 
But first clean up the local docker [container, image, volume, etc.]

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- code-simplifier:code-simplifier: Simplifies and refines code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. Focuses on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise. (Tools: All tools)

- docs-consistency-guard: Documentation agent that audits docs for staleness and inconsistency AND generates/updates documentation on request. Use proactively when reviewing any PR that touches CLAUDE.md, docs/adr/, CONTRIBUTING.md, or docs/architecture.md. Also invoke when the user asks "is the documentation up to date?", "update the milestone", "scaffold the ADR for X", "add this to the ADR index", "add a TODO item", "scaffold a README for X", or "create a doc stub for Y." (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Write, Edit)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- migration-safety: Specialized reviewer for Alembic migrations under backend/alembic/versions/. Audits for backward-incompatible operations that would break running code during a deploy, per working-agreement (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- orchestration-abstraction-guard: Specialized reviewer that audits backend code for provider-specific branching that bypasses the OrchestrationProvider abstraction. Use this proactively when reviewing any PR that touches backend/app/orchestration/, backend/app/services/, or backend/app/api/ — especially any code path handling ADF or Airflow events, trigger bindings, or pipeline_runs. Also invoke when the user asks "does this go through the abstraction?" or similar. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- pr-review-toolkit:code-reviewer: Use this agent when you need to review code for adherence to project guidelines, style guides, and best practices. This agent should be used proactively after writing or modifying code, especially before committing changes or creating pull requests. It will check for style violations, potential issues, and ensure code follows the established patterns in CLAUDE.md. Also the agent needs to know which files to focus on for the review. In most cases this will be recently completed work which is unstaged in git (can be retrieved by running git diff). However there can be cases where this is different, make sure to specify this as the agent input when calling the agent. Typical triggers include the user asking for a review of a feature they just implemented, the assistant proactively reviewing its own newly-written code before declaring a task done, and a final pre-PR check before opening a pull request. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:code-simplifier: Use this agent when code has been written or modified and needs to be simplified for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. This agent should be triggered automatically after completing a coding task or writing a logical chunk of code. It simplifies code by following project best practices while retaining all functionality. The agent focuses only on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise.

Examples:

<example>
Context: The assistant has just implemented a new feature that adds user authentication to an API endpoint.
user: "Please add authentication to the /api/users endpoint"
assistant: "I've implemented the authentication for the /api/users endpoint. Here's the code:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
Since a logical chunk of code has been written (authentication feature), use the Task tool to launch the code-simplifier agent to improve the code's clarity and maintainability while preserving functionality.
</commentary>
assistant: "Now let me use the code-simplifier agent to refine this implementation for better clarity and maintainability"
</example>

<example>
Context: The assistant has just fixed a bug by adding several conditional checks.
user: "Fix the null pointer exception in the data processor"
assistant: "I've added the necessary null checks to prevent the exception:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
After modifying code to fix a bug, use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the fix follows best practices and maintains code quality.
</commentary>
assistant: "Let me refine this bug fix using the code-simplifier agent to ensure it follows our best practices"
</example>

<example>
Context: The assistant has just refactored a function to improve performance.
user: "Optimize the data sorting algorithm for better performance"
assistant: "I've optimized the sorting algorithm. Here's the updated implementation:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
After completing a performance optimization task, use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the optimized code is also clear and maintainable.
</commentary>
assistant: "Now I'll use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the optimized code is also clear and follows our coding standards"
</example> (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:comment-analyzer: Use this agent when you need to analyze code comments for accuracy, completeness, and long-term maintainability. This includes (1) after generating large documentation comments or docstrings, (2) before finalizing a pull request that adds or modifies comments, (3) when reviewing existing comments for potential technical debt or comment rot, and (4) when you need to verify that comments accurately reflect the code they describe. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:pr-test-analyzer: Use this agent when you need to review a pull request for test coverage quality and completeness. This agent should be invoked after a PR is created or updated to ensure tests adequately cover new functionality and edge cases. Typical triggers include the user asking whether tests on a freshly-created PR are thorough, an updated PR adding new logic that needs coverage analysis, and a final pre-merge double-check before marking a PR ready. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:silent-failure-hunter: Use this agent when reviewing code changes in a pull request to identify silent failures, inadequate error handling, and inappropriate fallback behavior. This agent should be invoked proactively after completing a logical chunk of work that involves error handling, catch blocks, fallback logic, or any code that could potentially suppress errors. Examples:

<example>
Context: Daisy has just finished implementing a new feature that fetches data from an API with fallback behavior.
Daisy: "I've added error handling to the API client. Can you review it?"
Assistant: "Let me use the silent-failure-hunter agent to thoroughly examine the error handling in your changes."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example>

<example>
Context: Daisy has created a PR with changes that include try-catch blocks.
Daisy: "Please review PR #1234"
Assistant: "I'll use the silent-failure-hunter agent to check for any silent failures or inadequate error handling in this PR."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example>

<example>
Context: Daisy has just refactored error handling code.
Daisy: "I've updated the error handling in the authentication module"
Assistant: "Let me proactively use the silent-failure-hunter agent to ensure the error handling changes don't introduce silent failures."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example> (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:type-design-analyzer: Use this agent when you need expert analysis of type design in your codebase. Specifically use it (1) when introducing a new type to ensure it follows best practices for encapsulation and invariant expression, (2) during pull request creation to review all types being added, and (3) when refactoring existing types to improve their design quality. The agent will provide both qualitative feedback and quantitative ratings on encapsulation, invariant expression, usefulness, and enforcement. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

## claude.ai Context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## claude.ai Hugging Face
You have tools for using the Hugging Face Hub. arXiv paper id's are often used as references between datasets, models and papers. There are over 100 tags in use, common tags include 'Text Generation', 'Transformers', 'Image Classification' and so on.
Hugging Face tools are being used by authenticated user 'TheurgicDuke771'

## claude.ai Microsoft Learn
# Microsoft Learn MCP Server

This server gives structured access to official Microsoft and Azure documentation via three tools:

## Tools

### microsoft_docs_search
Search official documentation and return up to 10 concise, high-quality content chunks (max 500 tokens each), including title, URL, and excerpt.

- Use first to get a quick, reliable overview
- Ideal for grounding answers in Microsoft knowledge

### microsoft_code_sample_search
Search for code snippets and examples in official Microsoft Learn documentation and return up to 20 relevant, high-quality code samples.

- Use when you need to provide sample Microsoft/Azure related code in your answers.  
- Ideal for generating code snippets or practical implementation examples.  
- Optional parameter `language` can filter results. 

### microsoft_docs_fetch
Fetch and convert full Microsoft documentation pages to markdown.

- Use after search when you need full content from a specific URL
- Required for detailed tutorials, troubleshooting, prerequisites, code samples, or when search results are incomplete or outdated

## Workflow

1. Use `microsoft_docs_search` to find relevant documents.
2. If you need code examples or practical snippets, use `microsoft_code_sample_search`.
3. If deeper or complete information is needed, use `microsoft_docs_fetch`.

**Search gives breadth. Code Sample Search gives practical examples. Fetch gives depth.**

All content comes from Microsoft Learn or official sources, returned in clean markdown format.

## claude.ai Postman
Read the instructions resource completely for detailed usage instructions before answering any API-related questions.

## context7
Use this server to retrieve up-to-date documentation and code examples for any library.

attachment.content:
- commit-commands:clean_gone: Cleans up all git branches marked as [gone] (branches that have been deleted on the remote but still exist locally), including removing associated worktrees.
- commit-commands:commit-push-pr: Commit, push, and open a PR
- commit-commands:commit: Create a git commit
- code-review:code-review: Code review a pull request
- pr-review-toolkit:review-pr: Comprehensive PR review using specialized agents
- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# Two env files (split in #209 so app Settings can run extra="forbid"):
2	#   .env      — infra/compose only: POSTGRES_* (+ VITE_* for the frontend). This is
3	#               the file compose auto-substitutes ${...} from. setup.sh creates it
4	#               from .env.example. NOT loaded into the api/worker containers.
5	#   .env.app  — app config (DATABASE_URL, AZURE_*, AUTH_DEV_BYPASS, SECRET_STORE, …):
6	#               only keys the app's Settings recognizes. Loaded into api/worker/
7	#               migrate via env_file below; created from .env.app.example.
8	# DATABASE_URL is identical across migrate/api/worker. Postgres user/password/db
9	# carry NO embedded defaults — they come from .env or the shell. The `:?` on the
10	# canonical postgres block below makes compose fail fast if any is unset, rather
11	# than baking credentials into the file.
12	x-database-url: &database-url "REDACTED"
13	
14	services:
15	  postgres:
16	    image: postgres:16-alpine
17	    restart: unless-stopped
18	    environment:
19	      POSTGRES_USER: "${POSTGRES_USER:?set POSTGRES_USER in .env}"
20	      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: "${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:?set POSTGRES_PASSWORD in .env}"
21	      POSTGRES_DB: "${POSTGRES_DB:?set POSTGRES_DB in .env}"
22	    ports:
23	      - "5432:5432"
24	    volumes:
25	      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
26	    healthcheck:
27	      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER}"]
28	      interval: 5s
29	      timeout: 3s
30	      retries: 10
31	
32	  redis:
33	    image: redis:7-alpine
34	    restart: unless-stopped
35	    ports:

file.content:
# Two env files (split in #209 so app Settings can run extra="forbid"):
#   .env      — infra/compose only: POSTGRES_* (+ VITE_* for the frontend). This is
#               the file compose auto-substitutes ${...} from. setup.sh creates it
#               from .env.example. NOT loaded into the api/worker containers.
#   .env.app  — app config (DATABASE_URL, AZURE_*, AUTH_DEV_BYPASS, SECRET_STORE, …):
#               only keys the app's Settings recognizes. Loaded into api/worker/
#               migrate via env_file below; created from .env.app.example.
# DATABASE_URL is identical across migrate/api/worker. Postgres user/password/db
# carry NO embedded defaults — they come from .env or the shell. The `:?` on the
# canonical postgres block below makes compose fail fast if any is unset, rather
# than baking credentials into the file.
x-database-url: &database-url "REDACTED"

services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: "${POSTGRES_USER:?set POSTGRES_USER in .env}"
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: "${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:?set POSTGRES_PASSWORD in .env}"
      POSTGRES_DB: "${POSTGRES_DB:?set POSTGRES_DB in .env}"
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER}"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10

  # One-shot: applies Alembic migrations then exits 0. api/worker wait on it via
  # service_completed_successfully so `docker compose up` never serves an
  # un-migrated schema (#156). Mirrors setup.sh's `cd backend && alembic ...`
  # because alembic.ini's script_location is relative to backend/.
  migrate:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: backend/Dockerfile
    restart: "no"
    command: sh -c "cd backend && alembic upgrade head"
    env_file:
      - path: .env.app
        required: false
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: *database-url
    volumes:
      - ./backend:/workspace/backend
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

  # FastAPI + Celery share one image (backend/Dockerfile, conda env `dataq`).
  # DATABASE_URL / REDIS_URL are overridden here to the in-network service
  # hostnames (the .env defaults point at localhost for host-side dev).
  api:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: backend/Dockerfile
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: uvicorn backend.app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --reload
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    env_file:
      - path: .env.app
        required: false
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: *database-url
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/0
      # Shared across api + worker so a credential the API writes at runtime is
      # readable by connection-driven worker runs (#86). Dev only — plaintext in
      # Redis. NOTE: in redis mode KV_SECRET_* env vars are NOT consulted (that's
      # `env` mode); set SECRET_STORE=env if you rely on env-provided creds.
      SECRET_STORE: ${SECRET_STORE:-redis}
    volumes:
      # Bind-mount source for hot reload; the conda env in the image is untouched.
      - ./backend:/workspace/backend
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  worker:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: backend/Dockerfile
    restart: unless-stopped
    # -B runs celery-beat embedded in the worker (the #171 orchestration polling
    # schedule). Fine for the single-worker dev stack; prod runs a separate beat.
    command: celery -A backend.app.worker.celery_app worker -B --loglevel=INFO
    env_file:
      - path: .env.app
        required: false
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: *database-url
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/0
      # Must match the api service so secrets the API writes are visible here (#86).
      SECRET_STORE: ${SECRET_STORE:-redis}
    volumes:
      - ./backend:/workspace/backend
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  # React/Vite dev server. corepack pins pnpm from package.json's packageManager.
  # node_modules is an anonymous volume so the host's (possibly macOS-built) copy
  # doesn't clobber the container's. pnpm's ~300MB content-addressable store is
  # relocated to /pnpm-store (OUTSIDE the ./frontend bind) and held in its own
  # volume — so nothing leaks into, or creates an empty mountpoint under, the host
  # working tree. The browser loads from localhost:3000 and the Vite proxy forwards
  # /api + /mcp to the api service over the compose network.
  frontend:
    image: node:24-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    working_dir: /app
    command: sh -c "corepack enable && pnpm config set store-dir /pnpm-store && pnpm install && pnpm dev --host"
    environment:
      VITE_API_PROXY_TARGET: http://api:8000
      # Pass the client-side auth vars into the Vite dev server. Only VITE_*
      # vars are forwarded (DB/secret env stays out of the node container).
      # Substituted from the project .env; dev-bypass defaults on for local dev.
      VITE_AUTH_DEV_BYPASS: ${VITE_AUTH_DEV_BYPASS:-true}
      VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID: ${VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID:-}
      VITE_AZURE_SPA_CLIENT_ID: ${VITE_AZURE_SPA_CLIENT_ID:-}
      VITE_AZURE_API_CLIENT_ID: ${VITE_AZURE_API_CLIENT_ID:-}
      VITE_AZURE_API_SCOPE: ${VITE_AZURE_API_SCOPE:-user_impersonation}
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    volumes:
      - ./frontend:/app
      - /app/node_modules
      - pnpm-store:/pnpm-store
    depends_on:
      - api

volumes:
  postgres_data:
  pnpm-store:

content.text:
Here's the current Docker state, and there's a meaningful split I want to confirm before deleting anything:

**Clearly DataQ compose stack** (safe to remove — rebuildable from the repo):
- Containers: `dataq-{frontend,worker,api,migrate,postgres,redis}-1`
- Images: `dataq-api`, `dataq-worker`, `dataq-migrate`
- Volumes: `dataq_postgres_data` (dev seed data), `dataq_pnpm-store`
- Network: `dataq_default`

**DataQ-harness demo images** (the ADR 0021 live demo env — large, rebuilt via the harness, not this repo):
- `dataqacr3erlgd.azurecr.io/airflow-dataq` (10 tags, ~443MB–2.18GB each), `.../mockdata`, plus local `airflow-dataq:latest`, `mockdata:latest`

**NOT DataQ — must not touch:**
- `jellyfin` container + image (your media server)
- `docs_default` network (some other project)
- 14 anonymous hex volumes (unknown ownership — some likely jellyfin's)

So a blanket `docker system prune -a --volumes` is off the table — it'd kill jellyfin and the harness images. Two decisions for you:

questions.question: How wide should the cleanup go?

options.label: DataQ stack only (Recommended)
options.description: Remove only the dataq compose containers, dataq-* images, dataq network. Leaves harness airflow/mockdata images, jellyfin, and unknown volumes untouched.

options.label: DataQ stack + harness images
options.description: Also remove the dataqacr3erlgd.azurecr.io/airflow-dataq (10 tags) + mockdata images and local airflow-dataq/mockdata. Frees several GB but you'll rebuild them via the harness next time you run the demo env.

questions.question: What about the dataq named volumes (postgres dev data + pnpm store)?

options.label: Remove them too (Recommended)
options.description: docker compose down -v — drops dataq_postgres_data (dev seed/runs) and dataq_pnpm-store. Full clean; setup.sh re-seeds on next local run.

options.label: Keep the volumes
options.description: Stop/remove containers but preserve dataq_postgres_data and dataq_pnpm-store so local dev data survives.

lastPrompt: Get started with Week 7. Lets go to cloud.  But first clean up the local docker [container, image, volume, etc.]

1	# DataQ — deployment scaffolding
2	
3	Apply-ready Azure deploy scaffolding (Week 7). **Nothing here deploys on its own**
4	— the workflow ([.github/workflows/deploy.yml](../.github/workflows/deploy.yml)) is
5	`workflow_dispatch`-only until the Azure resources exist and the secrets/vars
6	below are set. This documents what to provision and how the pieces fit.
7	
8	Azure is **one** deploy target behind the app's seams (ADR 0010/0013) — the
9	manifests here are infra config, not business logic. No Azure resource names are
10	hardcoded in app code; they live only as workflow `vars`/`secrets`.
11	
12	## Topology
13	
14	```
15	Browser ─► Azure Static Web App (frontend/dist)
16	              │  /api/* proxied to the linked backend (same-origin → no CORS)
17	              ▼
18	        Azure Container Apps
19	          • dataq-api     (FastAPI image, external ingress :8000)
20	          • dataq-worker  (same image, `celery -A ... worker` + beat)
21	          • dataq-migrate (Container Apps Job: `alembic upgrade head`)
22	              │
23	              ├─► Azure Database for PostgreSQL (DATABASE_URL)
24	              ├─► Azure Cache for Redis        (REDIS_URL)
25	              ├─► Azure Key Vault              (SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault, managed identity)
26	              └─► Application Insights         (APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING)
27	        Azure Container Registry (ACR) — holds the backend image
28	```
29	
30	Both api + worker run the **same** image ([backend/Dockerfile](../backend/Dockerfile),
31	build context = repo root). The frontend is built and uploaded to SWA by the
32	workflow (the [frontend/Dockerfile](../frontend/Dockerfile) is the container
33	alternative — only needed if you run the UI on Container Apps instead of SWA).
34	
35	## One-time provisioning
36	
37	The datasource + compute infra is stood up by the external Terraform harness
38	(ADR 0021) — see `HARNESS_TODO.md`. Beyond that, this app needs:
39	
40	1. **ACR** + an **ACA environment** + the three apps/job above. The api/worker run
41	   `uvicorn …` / `celery …`; the migrate **job** runs `alembic upgrade head`.
42	2. **Managed identity** on the api + worker apps with **Key Vault Secrets User**
43	   on the vault (so `DefaultAzureCredential` resolves `SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault`).
44	3. **App env**: set the keys in [deploy/.env.app.prod.example](.env.app.prod.example)
45	   on the api + worker apps. Secret values (DB/Redis URL, App Insights) are Key
46	   Vault-backed Container Apps secrets — never literals.
47	4. **SWA linked backend**: link the `dataq-api` Container App as the SWA backend so
48	   `/api/*` is proxied same-origin (then `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS` can stay empty). If
49	   instead the SPA calls the API cross-origin, set `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS` to the SWA
50	   origin (the FastAPI CORS middleware turns on only when it's non-empty).
51	5. **Azure Monitor → ADF webhook** alert rule (Week-7 task) — needs the deployed
52	   API URL; configure after the first deploy.
53	
54	## GitHub config the workflow reads
55	
56	Set under repo **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions**, and add a
57	federated credential for OIDC login (subject = this repo's `production`
58	environment).
59	
60	**Secrets:** `AZURE_CLIENT_ID`, `AZURE_TENANT_ID`, `AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID`
61	(OIDC app registration), `AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN`.
62	
63	**Variables:** `ACR_NAME`, `ACR_LOGIN_SERVER`, `AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP`,
64	`API_APP_NAME`, `WORKER_APP_NAME`, `MIGRATE_JOB_NAME`, and the non-secret
65	`VITE_AZURE_*` build values.
66	
67	## Going live
68	
69	1. Provision the resources + set the secrets/vars above.
70	2. Run the **Deploy** workflow manually (`workflow_dispatch`) to validate end-to-end.
71	3. To deploy on every merge, uncomment the `push: branches: [main]` trigger in
72	   the workflow.
73	
74	Migrations are additive/backward-compatible (CLAUDE.md), so the workflow runs
75	`alembic upgrade head` **before** rolling the apps — the running old code
76	tolerates the new schema. (This is exactly the dev-DB step that, when skipped,
77	500s the checks endpoint after a schema-adding deploy.)

file.content:
# DataQ — deployment scaffolding

Apply-ready Azure deploy scaffolding (Week 7). **Nothing here deploys on its own**
— the workflow ([.github/workflows/deploy.yml](../.github/workflows/deploy.yml)) is
`workflow_dispatch`-only until the Azure resources exist and the secrets/vars
below are set. This documents what to provision and how the pieces fit.

Azure is **one** deploy target behind the app's seams (ADR 0010/0013) — the
manifests here are infra config, not business logic. No Azure resource names are
hardcoded in app code; they live only as workflow `vars`/`secrets`.

## Topology

```
Browser ─► Azure Static Web App (frontend/dist)
              │  /api/* proxied to the linked backend (same-origin → no CORS)
              ▼
        Azure Container Apps
          • dataq-api     (FastAPI image, external ingress :8000)
          • dataq-worker  (same image, `celery -A ... worker` + beat)
          • dataq-migrate (Container Apps Job: `alembic upgrade head`)
              │
              ├─► Azure Database for PostgreSQL (DATABASE_URL)
              ├─► Azure Cache for Redis        (REDIS_URL)
              ├─► Azure Key Vault              (SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault, managed identity)
              └─► Application Insights         (APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING)
        Azure Container Registry (ACR) — holds the backend image
```

Both api + worker run the **same** image ([backend/Dockerfile](../backend/Dockerfile),
build context = repo root). The frontend is built and uploaded to SWA by the
workflow (the [frontend/Dockerfile](../frontend/Dockerfile) is the container
alternative — only needed if you run the UI on Container Apps instead of SWA).

## One-time provisioning

The datasource + compute infra is stood up by the external Terraform harness
(ADR 0021) — see `HARNESS_TODO.md`. Beyond that, this app needs:

1. **ACR** + an **ACA environment** + the three apps/job above. The api/worker run
   `uvicorn …` / `celery …`; the migrate **job** runs `alembic upgrade head`.
2. **Managed identity** on the api + worker apps with **Key Vault Secrets User**
   on the vault (so `DefaultAzureCredential` resolves `SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault`).
3. **App env**: set the keys in [deploy/.env.app.prod.example](.env.app.prod.example)
   on the api + worker apps. Secret values (DB/Redis URL, App Insights) are Key
   Vault-backed Container Apps secrets — never literals.
4. **SWA linked backend**: link the `dataq-api` Container App as the SWA backend so
   `/api/*` is proxied same-origin (then `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS` can stay empty). If
   instead the SPA calls the API cross-origin, set `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS` to the SWA
   origin (the FastAPI CORS middleware turns on only when it's non-empty).
5. **Azure Monitor → ADF webhook** alert rule (Week-7 task) — needs the deployed
   API URL; configure after the first deploy.

## GitHub config the workflow reads

Set under repo **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions**, and add a
federated credential for OIDC login (subject = this repo's `production`
environment).

**Secrets:** `AZURE_CLIENT_ID`, `AZURE_TENANT_ID`, `AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID`
(OIDC app registration), `AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN`.

**Variables:** `ACR_NAME`, `ACR_LOGIN_SERVER`, `AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP`,
`API_APP_NAME`, `WORKER_APP_NAME`, `MIGRATE_JOB_NAME`, and the non-secret
`VITE_AZURE_*` build values.

## Going live

1. Provision the resources + set the secrets/vars above.
2. Run the **Deploy** workflow manually (`workflow_dispatch`) to validate end-to-end.
3. To deploy on every merge, uncomment the `push: branches: [main]` trigger in
   the workflow.

Migrations are additive/backward-compatible (CLAUDE.md), so the workflow runs
`alembic upgrade head` **before** rolling the apps — the running old code
tolerates the new schema. (This is exactly the dev-DB step that, when skipped,
500s the checks endpoint after a schema-adding deploy.)

1	name: Deploy
2	
3	# Manual-only for now (workflow_dispatch): apply-ready scaffolding that never
4	# fires on its own until the Azure resources exist + secrets/vars are set. Once
5	# the infra is provisioned (see deploy/README.md), flip on the push trigger:
6	#   push:
7	#     branches: [main]
8	on:
9	  workflow_dispatch:
10	    inputs:
11	      image_tag:
12	        description: "Image tag to build + deploy"
13	        default: "latest"
14	
15	concurrency:
16	  group: deploy-production
17	  cancel-in-progress: false
18	
19	# OIDC federated login to Azure (no stored client secret). The federated cred +
20	# the listed vars/secrets must be configured first — see deploy/README.md.
21	permissions:
22	  id-token: write
23	  contents: read
24	
25	env:
26	  IMAGE: ${{ vars.ACR_LOGIN_SERVER }}/dataq-backend:${{ inputs.image_tag }}
27	
28	jobs:
29	  backend:
30	    name: Backend — build, migrate, deploy (API + worker)
31	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
32	    environment: production
33	    steps:
34	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
35	
36	      - name: Azure login (OIDC)
37	        uses: azure/login@v2
38	        with:
39	          client-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_ID }}
40	          tenant-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
41	          subscription-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID }}
42	
43	      - name: Build + push backend image to ACR
44	        run: |
45	          az acr login --name "${{ vars.ACR_NAME }}"
46	          docker build -f backend/Dockerfile -t "$IMAGE" .
47	          docker push "$IMAGE"
48	
49	      # Migrate BEFORE rolling the app: migrations are additive/backward-compatible
50	      # (CLAUDE.md), so the running old code tolerates the new schema. Runs the
51	      # one-off migrate Container Apps Job (provisioned per deploy/README.md).
52	      # `az containerapp job start` only *starts* the execution and returns, so we
53	      # capture the execution name and poll it to a terminal state — the rollout
54	      # below must not begin until the migration has actually SUCCEEDED, and a
55	      # failed migration must abort the deploy (fail-closed) rather than roll the
56	      # apps onto an un-migrated schema.
57	      - name: Run DB migrations (alembic upgrade head) and wait for success
58	        run: |
59	          set -euo pipefail
60	          exec_name=$(az containerapp job start \
61	            --name "${{ vars.MIGRATE_JOB_NAME }}" \
62	            --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
63	            --image "$IMAGE" \
64	            --query name -o tsv)
65	          echo "Started migration execution: $exec_name"
66	          deadline=$(( SECONDS + 900 ))  # 15-minute cap
67	          while (( SECONDS < deadline )); do
68	            status=$(az containerapp job execution list \
69	              --name "${{ vars.MIGRATE_JOB_NAME }}" \
70	              --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
71	              --query "[?name=='$exec_name'].properties.status | [0]" -o tsv)
72	            echo "migration status: ${status:-<pending>}"
73	            case "$status" in
74	              Succeeded) echo "Migration succeeded."; exit 0 ;;
75	              Failed|Degraded|Stopped) echo "::error::Migration $status — aborting before app rollout."; exit 1 ;;
76	            esac
77	            sleep 10
78	          done
79	          echo "::error::Timed out waiting for migration to complete."; exit 1
80	
81	      - name: Deploy API
82	        run: |
83	          az containerapp update \
84	            --name "${{ vars.API_APP_NAME }}" \
85	            --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
86	            --image "$IMAGE"
87	
88	      - name: Deploy worker
89	        run: |
90	          az containerapp update \
91	            --name "${{ vars.WORKER_APP_NAME }}" \
92	            --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
93	            --image "$IMAGE"
94	
95	  frontend:
96	    name: Frontend — build + deploy to Static Web App
97	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
98	    environment: production
99	    steps:
100	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
101	      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
102	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
103	        with:
104	          node-version: "22"
105	          cache: pnpm
106	          cache-dependency-path: frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml
107	
108	      - name: Build
109	        working-directory: frontend
110	        # VITE_* are inlined at build time. Non-secret azure client/tenant ids
111	        # come from repo vars; never the SPA secret (there is none — MSAL is public).
112	        env:
113	          VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
114	          VITE_AZURE_SPA_CLIENT_ID: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_SPA_CLIENT_ID }}
115	          VITE_AZURE_API_CLIENT_ID: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_API_CLIENT_ID }}
116	          VITE_AZURE_API_SCOPE: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_API_SCOPE }}
117	          VITE_AUTH_DEV_BYPASS: "false"
118	        run: |
119	          pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
120	          pnpm build
121	
122	      - name: Deploy to Azure Static Web Apps
123	        uses: Azure/static-web-apps-deploy@v1
124	        with:
125	          azure_static_web_apps_api_token: ${{ secrets.AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN }}
126	          action: upload
127	          app_location: frontend/dist
128	          skip_app_build: true

file.content:
name: Deploy

# Manual-only for now (workflow_dispatch): apply-ready scaffolding that never
# fires on its own until the Azure resources exist + secrets/vars are set. Once
# the infra is provisioned (see deploy/README.md), flip on the push trigger:
#   push:
#     branches: [main]
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      image_tag:
        description: "Image tag to build + deploy"
        default: "latest"

concurrency:
  group: deploy-production
  cancel-in-progress: false

# OIDC federated login to Azure (no stored client secret). The federated cred +
# the listed vars/secrets must be configured first — see deploy/README.md.
permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read

env:
  IMAGE: ${{ vars.ACR_LOGIN_SERVER }}/dataq-backend:${{ inputs.image_tag }}

jobs:
  backend:
    name: Backend — build, migrate, deploy (API + worker)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Azure login (OIDC)
        uses: azure/login@v2
        with:
          client-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_ID }}
          tenant-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
          subscription-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID }}

      - name: Build + push backend image to ACR
        run: |
          az acr login --name "${{ vars.ACR_NAME }}"
          docker build -f backend/Dockerfile -t "$IMAGE" .
          docker push "$IMAGE"

      # Migrate BEFORE rolling the app: migrations are additive/backward-compatible
      # (CLAUDE.md), so the running old code tolerates the new schema. Runs the
      # one-off migrate Container Apps Job (provisioned per deploy/README.md).
      # `az containerapp job start` only *starts* the execution and returns, so we
      # capture the execution name and poll it to a terminal state — the rollout
      # below must not begin until the migration has actually SUCCEEDED, and a
      # failed migration must abort the deploy (fail-closed) rather than roll the
      # apps onto an un-migrated schema.
      - name: Run DB migrations (alembic upgrade head) and wait for success
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          exec_name=$(az containerapp job start \
            --name "${{ vars.MIGRATE_JOB_NAME }}" \
            --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
            --image "$IMAGE" \
            --query name -o tsv)
          echo "Started migration execution: $exec_name"
          deadline=$(( SECONDS + 900 ))  # 15-minute cap
          while (( SECONDS < deadline )); do
            status=$(az containerapp job execution list \
              --name "${{ vars.MIGRATE_JOB_NAME }}" \
              --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
              --query "[?name=='$exec_name'].properties.status | [0]" -o tsv)
            echo "migration status: ${status:-<pending>}"
            case "$status" in
              Succeeded) echo "Migration succeeded."; exit 0 ;;
              Failed|Degraded|Stopped) echo "::error::Migration $status — aborting before app rollout."; exit 1 ;;
            esac
            sleep 10
          done
          echo "::error::Timed out waiting for migration to complete."; exit 1

      - name: Deploy API
        run: |
          az containerapp update \
            --name "${{ vars.API_APP_NAME }}" \
            --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
            --image "$IMAGE"

      - name: Deploy worker
        run: |
          az containerapp update \
            --name "${{ vars.WORKER_APP_NAME }}" \
            --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
            --image "$IMAGE"

  frontend:
    name: Frontend — build + deploy to Static Web App
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "22"
          cache: pnpm
          cache-dependency-path: frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml

      - name: Build
        working-directory: frontend
        # VITE_* are inlined at build time. Non-secret azure client/tenant ids
        # come from repo vars; never the SPA secret (there is none — MSAL is public).
        env:
          VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
          VITE_AZURE_SPA_CLIENT_ID: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_SPA_CLIENT_ID }}
          VITE_AZURE_API_CLIENT_ID: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_API_CLIENT_ID }}
          VITE_AZURE_API_SCOPE: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_API_SCOPE }}
          VITE_AUTH_DEV_BYPASS: "false"
        run: |
          pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
          pnpm build

      - name: Deploy to Azure Static Web Apps
        uses: Azure/static-web-apps-deploy@v1

content.text:
Key findings before I provision anything:

**1. The deploy scaffolding is stale vs. ADR 0023.** The most recent commit (1959b8d) accepted **GHCR** as the registry and explicitly *"supersedes the #379 ACR deploy scaffolding"* — but [deploy.yml](.github/workflows/deploy.yml) and [deploy/README.md](deploy/README.md) still build/push to **ACR** (`az acr login`, `ACR_LOGIN_SERVER`, `ACR_NAME`). That reconciliation is an in-repo task, costs nothing, and should land before we wire up real infra.

**2. RP registration — the named blocker.** 4 of the providers we need are `NotRegistered`:

| Provider | State | Needed for |
|---|---|---|
| Microsoft.App | ✅ Registered | Container Apps |
| Microsoft.ContainerRegistry | ✅ Registered | (ACR — moot under GHCR) |
| Microsoft.OperationalInsights | ✅ Registered | ACA Log Analytics |
| Microsoft.DBforPostgreSQL | ✅ Registered | Postgres |
| **Microsoft.Web** | ❌ NotRegistered | Static Web App |
| **Microsoft.KeyVault** | ❌ NotRegistered | secrets |
| **Microsoft.Insights** | ❌ NotRegistered | App Insights |
| **Microsoft.Cache** | ❌ NotRegistered | Redis |

Registering a provider is free, reversible, and only needs the `register/action` permission (not full Owner). Let me test whether your account can clear the blocker directly:

input.command: for rp in Microsoft.Web Microsoft.KeyVault Microsoft.Insights Microsoft.Cache; do echo "registering $rp..."; az provider register -n $rp 2>&1 | head -2; done

file.content:
# ── Production app env reference (.env.app for prod) ──────────────────────────
# The prod values for backend Settings (backend/app/core/config.py). In Azure,
# set these as Container Apps env vars / secret refs on the api + worker apps —
# do NOT commit a filled-in copy. Secret VALUES live in Key Vault and are
# injected as Container Apps secrets; this file only documents the keys + shape.
# (Same discipline as .env.app.example: secret keys ship blank.)

ENVIRONMENT=prod
LOG_LEVEL=INFO

# Postgres (managed, e.g. Azure Database for PostgreSQL). Secret — inject the
# full URL (with password) as a Container Apps secret, not a literal here.
#   REDACTED
DATABASE_URL=
# Redis (managed). Secret — inject via Container Apps secret.
#   REDACTED
REDIS_URL=

# App Insights connection string (observability). Secret.
APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING=

SAMPLE_FAILURES_RETENTION_DAYS=30

# ── Azure AD SSO (real auth in prod — AUTH_DEV_BYPASS must be false) ──────────
AZURE_TENANT_ID=
AZURE_API_CLIENT_ID=
AZURE_SPA_CLIENT_ID=
AZURE_API_SCOPE=user_impersonation
AUTH_DEV_BYPASS=false

# Comma-separated workspace-admin emails.
WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS=

# ── Secret store: Key Vault in prod ──────────────────────────────────────────
SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault
# Vault URL (non-secret identifier). The app authenticates via
# DefaultAzureCredential (the Container App's managed identity needs Key Vault
# Secrets User on this vault).
AZURE_KEY_VAULT_URL=

# ── CORS: only if the SPA calls the API cross-origin ─────────────────────────
# Leave blank if the Static Web App proxies /api to the backend (same-origin —
# preferred). Set to the SPA origin(s) only if the SPA calls the API directly.
#   CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS=https://app.example.com
CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS=

# ── Webhook secret names (Key Vault keys, not values) ────────────────────────
REDACTED
REDACTED
# Workspace Teams webhook (the per-suite-webhook fallback). Unset = no Teams
# alerting unless a suite configures its own webhook. The VALUE (the URL) lives
# in Key Vault under this key; this is just the key name.
TEAMS_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME=

input.content:
# Week 7 — Go to cloud: DataQ app infra (Terraform) + GHCR deploy reconciliation

## Context

Week 6 is closed (27/27); we're entering **Week 7 — deployment, hardening & docs**.
The exit gate is *production-ready v1 deployed to Azure, CI/CD live*. Deploy
**scaffolding** (#379) exists but is incomplete and stale:

1. **Stale registry.** ADR 0023 (latest commit) made **GHCR** the registry and
   explicitly *supersedes the #379 ACR scaffolding* — but `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
   + `deploy/README.md` still build/push to **ACR** (`az acr login`, `ACR_*`). The
   ACA apps should pull `ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:<tag>` anonymously
   (public package → no registry credential, per ADR 0023).
2. **No app infra-as-code.** There is no Terraform for the *app's own* production
   resources. The harness Terraform (`~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/terraform/`,
   ADR 0021) stands up *datasources + demo env* only and must stay separate.

**Decisions (locked with the user):**
- Scope this round = author + reconcile + **apply** (provision billable infra now).
- New Terraform lives **in-repo** at `deploy/terraform/`; state gitignored.
- Redis = **self-hosted `redis:7-alpine` Container App** (internal, transient broker).
- GitHub Actions → Azure auth = **AAD app registration + OIDC** (federated cred).
- Reuse **subscription + RG `dataq-rg` only**; everything else is fresh `dataq-app-*`.
- Account is **Owner at subscription scope** → can register RPs + assign roles
  (the old "blocked on Owner" gate is cleared).

**Outcome:** DataQ v1 running on Azure Container Apps + Static Web App, backed by
managed Postgres, Key Vault, App Insights, and a self-hosted Redis broker — with a
GHCR-based CI deploy path wired for OIDC.

---

## Separation & tagging (hard constraint)

- All new resources: prefix `dataq-app-*`, tags
  `{ project = "dataq", managed = "terraform", purpose = "dataq-app" }`
  (mirrors harness `common_tags`, only `purpose` differs from `dataq-harness`).
- RG `dataq-rg` is **shared** — referenced, never destroyed. Its RG-level tag stays
  `project=dataq` (do not stamp `purpose` on the shared RG).
- The harness uses an `azurerm_resource_provider_registrations = "none"` SP; this
  stack applies as the **Owner user** so it can register RPs + grant roles.

---

## Part A — Reconcile deploy scaffolding to GHCR (ADR 0023)

In-repo edits, no cloud side effects:

- **`.github/workflows/deploy.yml`**
  - Drop the `az acr login` / `docker build`+`push` to ACR. Build + push to
    **GHCR** using `docker/login-action` with `${{ github.actor }}` /
    `${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}` (needs `permissions: packages: write`).
  - `env.IMAGE: ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:${{ inputs.image_tag }}`
    (lowercase owner — GHCR requirement).
  - Migrate-job + `containerapp update` steps keep their structure (the
    migrate-before-rollout gate + 15-min poll is correct — keep it).
  - Drop `ACR_NAME` / `ACR_LOGIN_SERVER` vars; keep RG/app-name vars.
- **`deploy/README.md`** — swap the ACR topology line + "GitHub config" section for
  GHCR (public package, anonymous ACA pull, no `AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN`
  change). Update the var list (remove `ACR_*`).
- **`deploy/.env.app.prod.example`** — already GHCR-agnostic; only fill in the
  vault/url shape notes if needed (no secret values).

---

## Part B — New Terraform at `deploy/terraform/`

Mirror harness conventions (local state, pinned providers, `local.common_tags`,
`random_string` suffix). Files:

| File | Contents |
|---|---|
| `versions.tf` | `terraform >= 1.9`; pins: `azurerm ~> 4`, `azuread ~> 3`, `random ~> 3.6`; **local backend** (`terraform.tfstate`, gitignored). |
| `providers.tf` | `azurerm { features {} }` (sub/tenant from `az login`/`ARM_*`), `azuread {}`, `random {}`. |
| `main.tf` | **Idempotent RG step** (see below) → `data "azurerm_resource_group"` → `local.common_tags` + `random_string.suffix`. |
| `variables.tf` | rg=`dataq-rg`, location=`West US 2`, `postgres_location=westus3` (wus2 offer-restricted per harness note), SKUs, `github_repo="TheurgicDuke771/DataQ"`, `backend_image` (GHCR), `image_tag`, `workspace_admin_emails`, Azure-AD SSO ids. |
| `rp.tf` | `azurerm_resource_provider_registration` for `Microsoft.Web`, `Microsoft.KeyVault`, `Microsoft.Insights`, `Microsoft.Cache` (the 4 NotRegistered). Declarative + idempotent. |
| `logs.tf` | `azurerm_log_analytics_workspace "dataq-app-logs"` (PerGB2018, 30d). |
| `appinsights.tf` | workspace-based `azurerm_application_insights "dataq-app-ai"`. |
| `identity.tf` | user-assigned `azurerm_user_assigned_identity "dataq-app-id"` for api+worker; `azurerm_role_assignment` → **Key Vault Secrets User** on the vault. |
| `keyvault.tf` | `azurerm_key_vault "dataq-app-kv-<suffix>"` (RBAC auth); secrets: `database-url`, `redis-url`, `appinsights-connection-string`, `adf-webhook-secret`, `airflow-webhook-secret`, (`teams-webhook` optional). Grant the **applier (Owner)** Key Vault Secrets Officer so TF can write secret values; grant the UAMI Secrets User (read). |
| `postgres.tf` | `azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server` (B1ms, v16, `westus3`, public + allow-Azure-services firewall) + `database "dataq"` + `random_password`. Build `DATABASE_URL` (`...?sslmode=require`) → KV secret. |
| `aca.tf` | `azurerm_container_app_environment "dataq-app-cae"` (→ logs workspace). |
| `redis.tf` | self-hosted `azurerm_container_app "dataq-app-redis"` — `redis:7-alpine`, internal TCP `:6379`, 1 replica. `REDIS_URL=redis://dataq-app-redis:6379/0` → KV secret. |
| `containerapps.tf` | `dataq-api` (external ingress :8000, uvicorn), `dataq-worker` (`celery … worker -B`), `dataq-migrate` **job** (`alembic upgrade head`). All: same GHCR image, UAMI attached, `SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault`, `AZURE_KEY_VAULT_URL`, secret refs (DATABASE_URL/REDIS_URL/APPINSIGHTS) as Container Apps secrets sourced from Key Vault via the UAMI, plus the SSO env from vars. |
| `swa.tf` | `azurerm_static_web_app "dataq-app-web"` (**Standard** — linked backends need Standard) + `azurerm_static_web_app_custom...`/linked-backend to `dataq-api` so `/api/*` is same-origin (CORS stays empty, per README §4 / ADR 0018). *Alt: Free + `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS`=SWA origin if cost matters.* |
| `github_oidc.tf` | `azuread_application` + `service_principal` + `azuread_application_federated_identity_credential` (subject `repo:TheurgicDuke771/DataQ:environment:production`) + `azurerm_role_assignment` Contributor on `dataq-rg`. |
| `outputs.tf` | api FQDN, SWA default hostname + **SWA api token** (sensitive), KV url, App Insights conn string (sensitive), and the GitHub config values: `AZURE_CLIENT_ID`/`AZURE_TENANT_ID`/`AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID`, app names, image ref. |

**Idempotent RG step (`main.tf`)** — user-requested:
```hcl
resource "null_resource" "ensure_rg" {
  provisioner "local-exec" {
    command = "az group create -n ${var.azure_resource_group} -l '${var.azure_location}' --tags project=dataq"
  }                       # az group create is idempotent; preserves project=dataq
}
data "azurerm_resource_group" "dataq" {
  name       = var.azure_resource_group
  depends_on = [null_resource.ensure_rg]
}
```

**`.gitignore`** — add `deploy/terraform/.terraform/`, `*.tfstate*`,
`*.tfvars` (keep `*.tfvars.example`), `.terraform.lock.hcl` kept tracked.

---

## Part C — Apply / bring-up sequence

1. **Build + push first backend image to GHCR** (chicken-and-egg: ACA needs an
   image to start). `gh auth token | docker login ghcr.io -u TheurgicDuke771 --password-stdin`;
   `docker build -f backend/Dockerfile -t ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:v1 .`;
   `docker push …`; **make the GHCR package public** (anonymous ACA pull, ADR 0023).
2. `terraform -chdir=deploy/terraform init && … plan` → **review** → `apply`.
   (RP registration first apply may take a few min.)
3. **Post-apply wiring** (mostly `gh` CLI from outputs):
   - Set repo **secrets** `AZURE_CLIENT_ID/TENANT_ID/SUBSCRIPTION_ID`,
     `AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN`; **vars** `AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP`,
     `API_APP_NAME`, `WORKER_APP_NAME`, `MIGRATE_JOB_NAME`, `VITE_AZURE_*`,
     GHCR image ref. Create the `production` GitHub environment.
   - Build + deploy the **frontend** to SWA (via the Deploy workflow or `swa` CLI).
4. **Verify** (below).
5. **Docs:** new ADR `0024` (app deploy infra: GHCR + ACA + self-hosted redis +
   SWA linked backend + OIDC app-reg); update CLAUDE.md §13 + `docs/progress.md`.

Deferred to later W7 tasks (not this round): Azure Monitor → ADF webhook alert
rule (needs the live API URL — wire after step 4), the FastMCP 8-tool server,
security hardening pass.

---

## Verification (end-to-end)

- `terraform plan` clean before apply; `apply` converges with no drift on re-run
  (idempotency check — run `plan` again, expect "No changes").
- `az resource list -g dataq-rg --query "[?tags.purpose=='dataq-app'].name"` lists
  exactly the new stack; harness `dataq-harness-*` untouched.
- **Migrate job**: `az containerapp job start --name dataq-migrate …` → execution
  `Succeeded`.
- **API health**: `curl https://<dataq-api fqdn>/api/v1/...` (health/openapi) 200;
  Swagger at `/docs`.
- **Secrets**: api/worker resolve `SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault` via the UAMI (no
  startup auth errors in `az containerapp logs show`).
- **Frontend**: SWA URL loads the SPA; `/api/*` proxies same-origin (no CORS error);
  Azure-AD login works (`AUTH_DEV_BYPASS=false`).
- **CI deploy**: trigger the **Deploy** workflow (`workflow_dispatch`) → OIDC login
  succeeds, builds+pushes GHCR image, migrate gate passes, api+worker roll.

---

## Risks / notes

- **GHCR package must be public** before apply or ACA pulls fail (ADR 0023 model).
- **SWA Standard** (~$9/mo) is required for the linked backend; Free+CORS is the
  cheaper fallback (flip `swa.tf` SKU + set `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS`).
- **AAD app registration** needs tenant app-reg rights — fine as Default Directory
  Owner; if blocked, fall back to a user-assigned identity + federated cred.
- Postgres in **westus3** (wus2 offer-restricted for this sub, per harness).
- Billing starts at apply: ACA env, Postgres B1ms, SWA Standard, Log Analytics,
  App Insights, KV. Self-hosted redis avoids managed-cache cost.
- Migrations are additive (CLAUDE.md) → migrate-before-rollout is safe.
- One-functionality-per-commit (CONTRIBUTING): land Part A (GHCR reconcile),
  Part B (terraform), and the ADR/docs as **separate commits/PRs**.

    **GHCR** using `docker/login-action` with `${{ github.actor }}` /
    `${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}` (needs `permissions: packages: write`).
  - `env.IMAGE: ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:${{ inputs.image_tag }}`
    (lowercase owner — GHCR requirement).
  - Migrate-job + `containerapp update` steps keep their structure (the
    migrate-before-rollout gate + 15-min poll is correct — keep it).
  - Drop `ACR_NAME` / `ACR_LOGIN_SERVER` vars; keep RG/app-name vars.
- **`deploy/README.md`** — swap the ACR topology line + "GitHub config" section for
  GHCR (public package, anonymous ACA pull, no `AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN`
  change). Update the var list (remove `ACR_*`).
- **`deploy/.env.app.prod.example`** — already GHCR-agnostic; only fill in the
  vault/url shape notes if needed (no secret values).

---

## Part B — New Terraform at `deploy/terraform/`

Mirror harness conventions (local state, pinned providers, `local.common_tags`,
`random_string` suffix). Files:

| File | Contents |
|---|---|
| `versions.tf` | `terraform >= 1.9`; pins: `azurerm ~> 4`, `azuread ~> 3`, `random ~> 3.6`; **local backend** (`terraform.tfstate`, gitignored). |
| `providers.tf` | `azurerm { features {} }` (sub/tenant from `az login`/`ARM_*`), `azuread {}`, `random {}`. |
| `main.tf` | **Idempotent RG step** (see below) → `data "azurerm_resource_group"` → `local.common_tags` + `random_string.suffix`. |
| `variables.tf` | rg=`dataq-rg`, location=`West US 2`, `postgres_location=westus3` (wus2 offer-restricted per harness note), SKUs, `github_repo="TheurgicDuke771/DataQ"`, `backend_image` (GHCR), `image_tag`, `workspace_admin_emails`, Azure-AD SSO ids. |
| `rp.tf` | `azurerm_resource_provider_registration` for `Microsoft.Web`, `Microsoft.KeyVault`, `Microsoft.Insights`, `Microsoft.Cache` (the 4 NotRegistered). Declarative + idempotent. |
| `logs.tf` | `azurerm_log_analytics_workspace "dataq-app-logs"` (PerGB2018, 30d). |
| `appinsights.tf` | workspace-based `azurerm_application_insights "dataq-app-ai"`. |
| `identity.tf` | user-assigned `azurerm_user_assigned_identity "dataq-app-id"` for api+worker; `azurerm_role_assignment` → **Key Vault Secrets User** on the vault. |
| `keyvault.tf` | `azurerm_key_vault "dataq-app-kv-<suffix>"` (RBAC auth); secrets: `database-url`, `redis-url`, `appinsights-connection-string`, `adf-webhook-secret`, `airflow-webhook-secret`, (`teams-webhook` optional). Grant the **applier (Owner)** Key Vault Secrets Officer so TF can write secret values; grant the UAMI Secrets User (read). |
| `postgres.tf` | `azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server` (B1ms, v16, `westus3`, public + allow-Azure-services firewall) + `database "dataq"` + `random_password`. Build `DATABASE_URL` (`...?sslmode=require`) → KV secret. |
| `aca.tf` | `azurerm_container_app_environment "dataq-app-cae"` (→ logs workspace). |
| `redis.tf` | self-hosted `azurerm_container_app "dataq-app-redis"` — `redis:7-alpine`, internal TCP `:6379`, 1 replica. `REDIS_URL=redis://dataq-app-redis:6379/0` → KV secret. |
| `containerapps.tf` | `dataq-api` (external ingress :8000, uvicorn), `dataq-worker` (`celery … worker -B`), `dataq-migrate` **job** (`alembic upgrade head`). All: same GHCR image, UAMI attached, `SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault`, `AZURE_KEY_VAULT_URL`, secret refs (DATABASE_URL/REDIS_URL/APPINSIGHTS) as Container Apps secrets sourced from Key Vault via the UAMI, plus the SSO env from vars. |
| `swa.tf` | `azurerm_static_web_app "dataq-app-web"` (**Standard** — linked backends need Standard) + `azurerm_static_web_app_custom...`/linked-backend to `dataq-api` so `/api/*` is same-origin (CORS stays empty, per README §4 / ADR 0018). *Alt: Free + `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS`=SWA origin if cost matters.* |
| `github_oidc.tf` | `azuread_application` + `service_principal` + `azuread_application_federated_identity_credential` (subject `repo:TheurgicDuke771/DataQ:environment:production`) + `azurerm_role_assignment` Contributor on `dataq-rg`. |
| `outputs.tf` | api FQDN, SWA default hostname + **SWA api token** (sensitive), KV url, App Insights conn string (sensitive), and the GitHub config values: `AZURE_CLIENT_ID`/`AZURE_TENANT_ID`/`AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID`, app names, image ref. |

**Idempotent RG step (`main.tf`)** — user-requested:
```hcl
resource "null_resource" "ensure_rg" {
  provisioner "local-exec" {
    command = "az group create -n ${var.azure_resource_group} -l '${var.azure_location}' --tags project=dataq"
  }                       # az group create is idempotent; preserves project=dataq
}
data "azurerm_resource_group" "dataq" {
  name       = var.azure_resource_group
  depends_on = [null_resource.ensure_rg]
}
```

**`.gitignore`** — add `deploy/terraform/.terraform/`, `*.tfstate*`,
`*.tfvars` (keep `*.tfvars.example`), `.terraform.lock.hcl` kept tracked.

---

## Part C — Apply / bring-up sequence

1. **Build + push first backend image to GHCR** (chicken-and-egg: ACA needs an
   image to start). `gh auth token | docker login ghcr.io -u TheurgicDuke771 --password-stdin`;
   `docker build -f backend/Dockerfile -t ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:v1 .`;
   `docker push …`; **make the GHCR package public** (anonymous ACA pull, ADR 0023).
2. `terraform -chdir=deploy/terraform init && … plan` → **review** → `apply`.
   (RP registration first apply may take a few min.)
3. **Post-apply wiring** (mostly `gh` CLI from outputs):
   - Set repo **secrets** `AZURE_CLIENT_ID/TENANT_ID/SUBSCRIPTION_ID`,
     `AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN`; **vars** `AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP`,
     `API_APP_NAME`, `WORKER_APP_NAME`, `MIGRATE_JOB_NAME`, `VITE_AZURE_*`,
     GHCR image ref. Create the `production` GitHub environment.
   - Build + deploy the **frontend** to SWA (via the Deploy workflow or `swa` CLI).
4. **Verify** (below).
5. **Docs:** new ADR `0024` (app deploy infra: GHCR + ACA + self-hosted redis +
   SWA linked backend + OIDC app-reg); update CLAUDE.md §13 + `docs/progress.md`.

Deferred to later W7 tasks (not this round): Azure Monitor → ADF webhook alert
rule (needs the live API URL — wire after step 4), the FastMCP 8-tool server,
security hardening pass.

---

## Verification (end-to-end)

- `terraform plan` clean before apply; `apply` converges with no drift on re-run
  (idempotency check — run `plan` again, expect "No changes").
- `az resource list -g dataq-rg --query "[?tags.purpose=='dataq-app'].name"` lists
  exactly the new stack; harness `dataq-harness-*` untouched.
- **Migrate job**: `az containerapp job start --name dataq-migrate …` → execution
  `Succeeded`.
- **API health**: `curl https://<dataq-api fqdn>/api/v1/...` (health/openapi) 200;
  Swagger at `/docs`.
- **Secrets**: api/worker resolve `SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault` via the UAMI (no
  startup auth errors in `az containerapp logs show`).
- **Frontend**: SWA URL loads the SPA; `/api/*` proxies same-origin (no CORS error);
  Azure-AD login works (`AUTH_DEV_BYPASS=false`).
- **CI deploy**: trigger the **Deploy** workflow (`workflow_dispatch`) → OIDC login
  succeeds, builds+pushes GHCR image, migrate gate passes, api+worker roll.

---

## Risks / notes

- **GHCR package must be public** before apply or ACA pulls fail (ADR 0023 model).
- **SWA Standard** (~$9/mo) is required for the linked backend; Free+CORS is the
  cheaper fallback (flip `swa.tf` SKU + set `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS`).
- **AAD app registration** needs tenant app-reg rights — fine as Default Directory
  Owner; if blocked, fall back to a user-assigned identity + federated cred.
- Postgres in **westus3** (wus2 offer-restricted for this sub, per harness).
- Billing starts at apply: ACA env, Postgres B1ms, SWA Standard, Log Analytics,
  App Insights, KV. Self-hosted redis avoids managed-cache cost.
- Migrations are additive (CLAUDE.md) → migrate-before-rollout is safe.
- One-functionality-per-commit (CONTRIBUTING): land Part A (GHCR reconcile),
  Part B (terraform), and the ADR/docs as **separate commits/PRs**.

**scaffolding** (#379) exists but is incomplete and stale:

1. **Stale registry.** ADR 0023 (latest commit) made **GHCR** the registry and
   explicitly *supersedes the #379 ACR scaffolding* — but `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
   + `deploy/README.md` still build/push to **ACR** (`az acr login`, `ACR_*`). The
   ACA apps should pull `ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:<tag>` anonymously
   (public package → no registry credential, per ADR 0023).
2. **No app infra-as-code.** There is no Terraform for the *app's own* production
   resources. The harness Terraform (`~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/terraform/`,
   ADR 0021) stands up *datasources + demo env* only and must stay separate.

**Decisions (locked with the user):**
- Scope this round = author + reconcile + **apply** (provision billable infra now).
- New Terraform lives **in-repo** at `deploy/terraform/`; state gitignored.
- Redis = **self-hosted `redis:7-alpine` Container App** (internal, transient broker).
- GitHub Actions → Azure auth = **AAD app registration + OIDC** (federated cred).
- Reuse **subscription + RG `dataq-rg` only**; everything else is fresh `dataq-app-*`.
- Account is **Owner at subscription scope** → can register RPs + assign roles
  (the old "blocked on Owner" gate is cleared).

**Outcome:** DataQ v1 running on Azure Container Apps + Static Web App, backed by
managed Postgres, Key Vault, App Insights, and a self-hosted Redis broker — with a
GHCR-based CI deploy path wired for OIDC.

| `identity.tf` | user-assigned `azurerm_user_assigned_identity "dataq-app-id"` for api+worker; `azurerm_role_assignment` → **Key Vault Secrets User** on the vault. |
| `keyvault.tf` | `azurerm_key_vault "dataq-app-kv-<suffix>"` (RBAC auth); secrets: `database-url`, `redis-url`, `appinsights-connection-string`, `adf-webhook-secret`, `airflow-webhook-secret`, (`teams-webhook` optional). Grant the **applier (Owner)** Key Vault Secrets Officer so TF can write secret values; grant the UAMI Secrets User (read). |
| `postgres.tf` | `azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server` (B1ms, v16, `westus3`, public + allow-Azure-services firewall) + `database "dataq"` + `random_password`. Build `DATABASE_URL` (`...?sslmode=require`) → KV secret. |
| `aca.tf` | `azurerm_container_app_environment "dataq-app-cae"` (→ logs workspace). |
| `redis.tf` | self-hosted `azurerm_container_app "dataq-app-redis"` — `redis:7-alpine`, internal TCP `:6379`, 1 replica. `REDIS_URL=redis://dataq-app-redis:6379/0` → KV secret. |
| `containerapps.tf` | `dataq-api` (external ingress :8000, uvicorn), `dataq-worker` (`celery … worker -B`), `dataq-migrate` **job** (`alembic upgrade head`). All: same GHCR image, UAMI attached, `SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault`, `AZURE_KEY_VAULT_URL`, secret refs (DATABASE_URL/REDIS_URL/APPINSIGHTS) as Container Apps secrets sourced from Key Vault via the UAMI, plus the SSO env from vars. |
| `swa.tf` | `azurerm_static_web_app "dataq-app-web"` (**Standard** — linked backends need Standard) + `azurerm_static_web_app_custom...`/linked-backend to `dataq-api` so `/api/*` is same-origin (CORS stays empty, per README §4 / ADR 0018). *Alt: Free + `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS`=SWA origin if cost matters.* |
| `github_oidc.tf` | `azuread_application` + `service_principal` + `azuread_application_federated_identity_credential` (subject `repo:TheurgicDuke771/DataQ:environment:production`) + `azurerm_role_assignment` Contributor on `dataq-rg`. |
| `outputs.tf` | api FQDN, SWA default hostname + **SWA api token** (sensitive), KV url, App Insights conn string (sensitive), and the GitHub config values: `AZURE_CLIENT_ID`/`AZURE_TENANT_ID`/`AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID`, app names, image ref. |

**Idempotent RG step (`main.tf`)** — user-requested:
```hcl
resource "null_resource" "ensure_rg" {
  provisioner "local-exec" {
    command = "az group create -n ${var.azure_resource_group} -l '${var.azure_location}' --tags project=dataq"
  }                       # az group create is idempotent; preserves project=dataq
}
data "azurerm_resource_group" "dataq" {
  name       = var.azure_resource_group
  depends_on = [null_resource.ensure_rg]
}
```

**`.gitignore`** — add `deploy/terraform/.terraform/`, `*.tfstate*`,
`*.tfvars` (keep `*.tfvars.example`), `.terraform.lock.hcl` kept tracked.

---

## Part C — Apply / bring-up sequence

1. **Build + push first backend image to GHCR** (chicken-and-egg: ACA needs an
   image to start). `gh auth token | docker login ghcr.io -u TheurgicDuke771 --password-stdin`;
   `docker build -f backend/Dockerfile -t ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:v1 .`;
   `docker push …`; **make the GHCR package public** (anonymous ACA pull, ADR 0023).
2. `terraform -chdir=deploy/terraform init && … plan` → **review** → `apply`.
   (RP registration first apply may take a few min.)
3. **Post-apply wiring** (mostly `gh` CLI from outputs):
   - Set repo **secrets** `AZURE_CLIENT_ID/TENANT_ID/SUBSCRIPTION_ID`,
     `AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN`; **vars** `AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP`,
     `API_APP_NAME`, `WORKER_APP_NAME`, `MIGRATE_JOB_NAME`, `VITE_AZURE_*`,
     GHCR image ref. Create the `production` GitHub environment.
   - Build + deploy the **frontend** to SWA (via the Deploy workflow or `swa` CLI).
4. **Verify** (below).
5. **Docs:** new ADR `0024` (app deploy infra: GHCR + ACA + self-hosted redis +
   SWA linked backend + OIDC app-reg); update CLAUDE.md §13 + `docs/progress.md`.

Deferred to later W7 tasks (not this round): Azure Monitor → ADF webhook alert
rule (needs the live API URL — wire after step 4), the FastMCP 8-tool server,
security hardening pass.

---

## Verification (end-to-end)

- `terraform plan` clean before apply; `apply` converges with no drift on re-run
  (idempotency check — run `plan` again, expect "No changes").
- `az resource list -g dataq-rg --query "[?tags.purpose=='dataq-app'].name"` lists
  exactly the new stack; harness `dataq-harness-*` untouched.
- **Migrate job**: `az containerapp job start --name dataq-migrate …` → execution
  `Succeeded`.
- **API health**: `curl https://<dataq-api fqdn>/api/v1/...` (health/openapi) 200;
  Swagger at `/docs`.
- **Secrets**: api/worker resolve `SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault` via the UAMI (no
  startup auth errors in `az containerapp logs show`).
- **Frontend**: SWA URL loads the SPA; `/api/*` proxies same-origin (no CORS error);
  Azure-AD login works (`AUTH_DEV_BYPASS=false`).
- **CI deploy**: trigger the **Deploy** workflow (`workflow_dispatch`) → OIDC login
  succeeds, builds+pushes GHCR image, migrate gate passes, api+worker roll.

---

## Risks / notes

- **GHCR package must be public** before apply or ACA pulls fail (ADR 0023 model).
- **SWA Standard** (~$9/mo) is required for the linked backend; Free+CORS is the
  cheaper fallback (flip `swa.tf` SKU + set `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS`).
- **AAD app registration** needs tenant app-reg rights — fine as Default Directory
  Owner; if blocked, fall back to a user-assigned identity + federated cred.
- Postgres in **westus3** (wus2 offer-restricted for this sub, per harness).
- Billing starts at apply: ACA env, Postgres B1ms, SWA Standard, Log Analytics,
  App Insights, KV. Self-hosted redis avoids managed-cache cost.
- Migrations are additive (CLAUDE.md) → migrate-before-rollout is safe.
- One-functionality-per-commit (CONTRIBUTING): land Part A (GHCR reconcile),
  Part B (terraform), and the ADR/docs as **separate commits/PRs**.
